<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I don't know about you, but I am someone who likes to settle into a mood, into a tone or a situation, into a liturgy, and feel it completely and fully.<|16.94|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Think of days like Pentecost or Christmas or Ash Wednesday, and even all of the days that are going to follow this week, culminating in Easter, all have a very distinct flavor and mood.<|17.50|>
<|18.26|> They allow us to be fully present to one set of emotions.<|23.86|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Palm Sunday does not.<|2.32|>
<|3.32|> It does not allow us to get comfortable.<|6.84|>
<|8.26|> This day does not allow us to settle in.<|11.56|>
<|12.98|> This day has us moving with lightning speed from across the street,<|18.46|>
<|19.06|> singing all glory, laud, and honor to the conclusion of this service<|23.80|>
<|23.80|> with, oh, sacred head, sore wounded.<|28.02|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> It's all the feelings.<|2.16|>
<|3.40|> We're shouting Hosanna one minute and demanding crucify him moments later.<|9.88|>
<|10.90|> It's jarring indeed.<|13.34|>
<|15.82|> And yet, isn't this our very life?<|19.00|>
<|19.00|> It's our very life.<|19.80|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Like the music at that funeral procession, we are a complex lot.<|6.56|>
<|7.60|> People of many moods, many whims, many emotions,<|13.76|>
<|14.44|> and often many of them at the same time or in rapid succession.<|19.68|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.62|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> At the end of our life, the soundtrack is not just going to be one song playing on repeat.<|11.38|>
<|12.30|> It will be a soundtrack, a playlist comprised of show tunes, the blues, maybe some outlaw country,<|24.40|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Bluegrass, classical, a true overture, varied and layered.<|8.62|>
<|9.84|> It will be jarring.<|11.28|>
<|12.68|> It will keep us changing gears, sometimes quickly.<|16.98|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> you<|1.12|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> I think Palm Sunday points to this, to our mercurial ways, Hosanna to crucify him, to the reality that we rarely stay in one mood, in one frame of mind for very long.<|19.10|><|endoftext|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And realizing this doesn't mean I like it anymore.<|3.84|>
<|5.40|> But I do have more tolerance for what it shows me about myself<|10.84|>
<|10.84|> and forcing me to be more honest about my own intemperate nature.<|18.20|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Thank you.<|1.80|><|endoftext|>

<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Come back every day and walk a little more slowly toward the cross.<|7.16|>
